title: PG&E Corporation (PCG) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of PG&E Corporation (PCG) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Projecting 5% FCF growth. As a regulated utility, PCG has virtually no traditional free cash flow due to massive CapEx for wildfire mitigation. This growth rate assumes a gradual stabilization of CapEx and steady rate base expansion authorized by the CPUC over the decade.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 7.5% discount rate is utilized. This reflects PCG's regulated monopoly status which lowers business risk, offset by the significant, ongoing idiosyncratic risks related to California wildfires and the associated political environment.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A conservative 2.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation and the mature, regulated nature of the utility business model in California.

thesisTitle: Regulated Growth Amidst High Capital Needs
thesisParagraphs: PG&E Corporation operates as a heavily regulated electric utility monopoly. While this guarantees a captive customer base, the company requires immense ongoing capital expenditures to harden its grid against wildfire risks, severely limiting actual free cash flow generation.

The valuation model assumes that as the most intensive phases of grid hardening conclude, CapEx will normalize relative to operating cash flow. Long-term value is highly dependent on favorable rate case outcomes from the CPUC to ensure an adequate return on its expanding asset base.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is PCG's Free Cash Flow negative?
faq.answer: PCG requires massive capital expenditures for infrastructure upgrades, particularly undergrounding lines for wildfire mitigation, which currently exceeds operating cash flow.

faq.question: How does regulation impact PCG's value?
faq.answer: The California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) sets the rates PCG can charge and the return it can earn on equity, placing a ceiling on profitability but ensuring baseline revenue stability.

faq.question: What is the primary risk to this valuation?
faq.answer: The primary risk is catastrophic wildfire liability or adverse regulatory rulings that prevent PCG from fully recovering its capital investments.
